title: Night Crystal Round
desc: The street crystals are coming on late, and UnderWorld City looks worse when it cannot see its own ruins.

steps.text:
UnderWorld City is at its most unsettling in the hour before the street crystals fully wake. The block-paved roads are all shadow and ember glow, the gargoyles above the rooflines look ready to move, and the broken vitraux in the old facades catch just enough light to remember that this place was once built to impress someone. Then the crystals brighten one by one and the city reveals itself again: damaged, crowded, and far too proud to admit either fact.

Tonight one whole stretch of the lower hill road is lagging behind. The lamplighter on duty hands you a polishing cloth and a tuning rod. "Upper brackets if you do not mind heights," she says. "Lower reflector pans if you prefer your danger closer to the ground. Either way, I want this district lit before the night crowd starts pretending it likes stumbling."

options.label: Climb the upper brackets and tune the street crystals
Work the lower reflector pans and clear the light path

steps.text:
The upper brackets jut from the old facades on iron arms blackened by soot and age. Up there the city opens beneath you in layers: crooked roofs, boarded windows, fortress lights on the hill, and long streets of dark stone waiting for their color to return. Most of the crystals are not dead, only dulled. Soot film, mineral haze, a mounting bolt turned half a notch off true. You clean, retune, and set each one until the red-orange cores take the charge properly and throw their glow back onto the paving below.

By the time you reach the last bracket, the whole hill road has shifted. What was murk becomes structure. Broken arches gain edges again. The colored glass still hanging in the windows starts answering the crystals with bruised reds and sulfur yellows. The lamplighter looks down the road, then up at the brackets you just cleared. "There," she says. "Ugly, yes. But at least now it is ugly on purpose."

options.label: Climb down and leave the district properly lit

steps.text:
The reflector pans sit low at the street corners, shallow metal dishes that catch crystal light and throw it under awnings, archways, and the mouths of side lanes where dark tends to gather first. Half the problem tonight is soot. The other half is loose angle pins that have let the pans drift until they are illuminating walls instead of roads. You clean each dish with black grit under your nails, reset the pins, and watch the light slide back where the foot traffic actually needs it.

It is humble work next to tuning the crystals themselves, but the effect spreads quickly. Alley mouths stop looking bottomless. Doorways stop turning strangers into silhouettes. Even the pavers seem less eager to trip anyone. When the lamplighter checks the corner line, the lower district has gone from threatening to merely UnderWorld. She seems content with that. "Good. Nobody here wants beauty. Visibility will do."

options.label: Stack the cleaning tools and let the street keep its shape
